title: Checker verdict
description: A checker's verdict on one guild task, written as JSON (the record of record) and rendered to a Markdown sibling for humans. Structured-output-safe by construction: no if/then, no $dynamicRef, no unevaluatedProperties, only the conservative keyword set (type, properties, required, items, enum, additionalProperties) — a second checker family writes this shape via strict vendor structured-output modes, which cannot evaluate conditionals. Two compromises this implies: (1) the two semantic rules a conditional would normally express here — a fail verdict must carry at least one finding, and every finding's evidence must be non-empty — are enforced by validate-verdict.py instead of by this schema; (2) 'blocked' is not a third flavor of outcome alongside pass/fail, it carries the guild's old ERROR semantics (the check itself couldn't complete — script crashed, tool unreachable, vendor quota hit) and does not count against the worker's retry budget.

task_id.description: The task this verdict checks, e.g. "T-001".

checker.description: The checker agent that produced this verdict, e.g. "checker-judgment".

vendor.description: The model vendor that ran the check, e.g. "anthropic".

model.description: The model that ran the check, e.g. "claude-opus-4".

clause_id.description: The constitution clause this finding is about, e.g. "C-1".

severity.description: How serious the finding is, e.g. "blocker", "major", "minor".

description.description: One sentence stating the defect or the clause being satisfied.

evidence.description: A file path plus line range (e.g. "foo.py:12-18"), or a command-output excerpt.

duration_ms.description: Optional: wall-clock duration of the check, in milliseconds.

cost_usd.description: Optional: the check's cost in US dollars.
